disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing chronic bladder pain condition (interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome), with recurrent flares every few months; this is the worst flare
trigger_question_examples: Have you had this kind of pain before?
Do you have any ongoing bladder or urinary conditions?
Is this a flare of something you've been diagnosed with?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I've had a chronic bladder pain condition for years, they diagnosed it after lots of tests. I get flares every few months but this one is by far the worst.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no shivering or rigors — patient has checked temperature at home and it is normal
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills, or shivering?
Have you felt hot and cold or sweaty and shaky?
Did you check your temperature — any sign of a fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever at all — I actually checked with our thermometer and it was normal. No shivering or chills, just the awful bladder pain.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't know, I've just been focused on the pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is localised to the suprapubic bladder area and lower abdomen; it does NOT radiate to the flank, loin, side, or back (no loin-to-groin colicky radiation)
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain spread to your back, side, or loin at all?
Is the pain only over your bladder, or does it move round to your back or flank?
Does it come in waves that travel from your back down to your groin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it stays right down low over my bladder and across my lower tummy. It doesn't go round to my back or my sides, and it's not coming in waves — it's a constant burning that's worst when my bladder fills.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It just hurts everywhere down there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is still passing urine — small volumes very frequently (every ~10 minutes), not in complete retention; no sense of a completely blocked or over-full bladder that will not empty
trigger_question_examples: Are you still able to pass urine, even small amounts?
Have you completely stopped being able to wee?
Does your bladder feel blocked and completely full without emptying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I am still weeing, but only a few drops each time and I'm going every ten minutes or so. It's not that I can't go at all — it's that it burns and there's barely anything there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has taken the maximum OTC dose of Ural and paracetamol today with no relief and describes desperation with a high risk of accidental overdose; psychological distress and pain-catastrophising are prominent
trigger_question_examples: Exactly what have you already taken today, and how much?
This sounds frightening and exhausting — how are you coping, and have you felt tempted to take more than the packet says?
How are you feeling in yourself with pain this severe — are you having any thoughts of harming yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken the full amount of Ural and the maximum paracetamol on the packet and it did nothing. Honestly I'm so desperate I keep thinking about just taking more to make it stop — I know I shouldn't, but I can't stand it.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I've just taken some painkillers, that's all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Standard analgesia (Ural, paracetamol) has been completely ineffective for this flare, consistent with a central/neuropathic pain mechanism rather than simple infection
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for the pain, and did it help?
What have you taken so far and did it make any difference?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've tried Ural and paracetamol and they've done absolutely nothing — the only thing that helps even a tiny bit is a hot water bottle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No abnormal vaginal discharge; no possibility of pregnancy relevant to management currently volunteered; symptoms are urinary/bladder rather than gynaecological
trigger_question_examples: Any unusual vaginal discharge or bleeding?
Is there any chance you could be pregnant?
Are there any symptoms lower down that aren't to do with your bladder?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No discharge or bleeding, nothing like that. This is all my bladder — the burning and the pressure and needing to wee constantly.
